title: Sampled faithfulness/entailment CI gate: cited source must actually support the claim (Galactica/ProVe failure defense)
context: PLAN.md v0.2 elevates faithfulness (does the cited source actually *entail* the claim?) to a first-class, separately-reported CI gate — distinct from citation-coverage (does a citation *exist*?). The Galactica failure (fluent text with real-looking but non-supporting citations) and the Wikidata ProVe failure (references that do not support their statements) are precisely what this gate defends against. Citation presence is necessary but not sufficient; a claim whose source does not entail it is as dangerous as an unsourced claim, and must be withheld or flagged. This is a safety-critical M1 subsystem: a bypass means faithfully-cited but unfaithful claims can reach the published synthesis.
objective: Implement a sampled faithfulness/entailment CI gate (packages/core/verify or packages/core/gates) that takes a claim + its cited source snapshot and judges whether the source *actually entails* the claim (using Anthropic Citations char-level spans as the primary signal, optionally an LLM-judge step); the gate runs in CI on a sampled set, fails the build if the entailment rate falls below the threshold (target ≥ 95% on sampled set), and reports faithfulness separately from citation-coverage so both are visible as distinct metrics.
acceptanceCriteria: The faithfulness gate is implemented as a distinct check from the citation-coverage test; a claim can pass citation-coverage (a source exists) but fail faithfulness (the source does not entail the claim), and vice versa — both are measured and reported independently

The gate uses Anthropic Citations char-level source spans as the primary entailment signal; unfaithful claims (below the entailment threshold) are withheld from the synthesis or flagged with a faithfulness-failure note in the provenance record
The gate runs in CI on a sampled set of published claims; it fails the build if the sampled entailment rate falls below the configured threshold (default target: ≥ 95%)
A human-calibration audit loop is documented: periodically a human reviewer spot-checks gate verdicts to calibrate the threshold and detect systematic entailment failures
The faithfulness gate is included in the red-team suite (atltuae-redteam-009) as a first-class refusal: a build that surfaces an unfaithful claim without flagging it fails CI
Reviewed and signed off by the gate reviewer + provenance auditor (recorded)
